title: Design a Form Submission Contract
summary: Build and verify form purpose method in cooling-center transport request intake through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain form purpose method from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply form purpose method without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 04 · Read the cooling-center transport request intake case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves form purpose method when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: cooling-center transport request intake.
steps.why: Design a Form Submission Contract makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in cooling-center transport request intake.

content.text: A form is a data contract. Its action identifies the receiving resource, its method expresses retrieval or state change, and each successful control contributes a name-value pair. GET suits repeatable searches whose parameters may appear in a URL. POST suits submissions that change state or contain data that should not be placed in a URL. Visible values without names are not submitted. Apply it to cooling-center transport request intake.
